rationale: Both candidates converge on the same discrepancy the ground-truth comment orbits (the description's trust_remote_code claim vs. the visible code), but neither reproduces the ground truth's precise concern (kernels<0.15.0 lacking that kwarg regressing hub loading), so recall is similarly partial for both. X pads out with a docstring nit and a test-confirmation ask that are grounded but low-value; Y adds a sharper, correctly-reasoned finding (global lock serializing FA2/FA3 loads) and reaches a verdict (APPROVE with non-blocking notes) that matches the actual outcome (LGTM/approved with one P2 comment) better than X's 'FOLLOW-UP REQUIRED'. Both are concrete and file/line-anchored, but Y's technical depth and calibration edge it out.
